# Feature: Web Expert Agent — Persona & Response Structure Overhaul

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-WEX-002
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Reshape the Web Expert Agent's persona and response template so it serves a wider audience well — engineers who want spec-level depth, and QA / PMs / non-engineers who want plain-English answers — without losing authority. Today the prompt is uniformly deep and academic; PMs/QA find it overwhelming, and engineers occasionally get fluff before substance.

This feature edits **only the system prompt** (now inlined into [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) by FEAT-FP-WEX-001). It introduces:

1. **Three response depths** — `Quick`, `Default`, `Deep` — with rules for when each applies and how to switch between them. (Command wiring for `/eli5` and `/deep` lands in FEAT-FP-WEX-004; this feature defines the *prompt-side* behavior they trigger.)
2. **A standard 5-section response layout** — TL;DR → Answer → Example → Spec reference → Key Takeaway — replacing the current loose "end with Key Takeaway blockquote" rule.
3. **Audience-tier defaults** — assume software professional with general web literacy; downshift on PM/QA-style cues, upshift on spec-citing cues.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (TypeScript strict, naming, async/await, semicolons, Vitest+RTL) are in [.spec-lite/memory.md](.spec-lite/memory.md). Only spec-specific notes below.

### Approach

- All edits land in the inlined prompt constant in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts) (the constant inlined by FEAT-FP-WEX-001 — likely named `WEB_EXPERT_SYSTEM_PROMPT_INLINE`).
- **Add a new section `## Audience & Depth Tiers`** between the existing `## Identity` and `## Expertise Domains` sections. Define three depths (Quick / Default / Deep), the cues that select each, and the explicit `/eli5` and `/deep` overrides.
- **Replace the existing `## Response Structure` section** with a stricter 5-section template (TL;DR → Answer → Example → Spec ref → Key Takeaway). Define what each section MUST contain and when sections can be omitted (e.g., Example may be omitted for non-code questions; Spec ref may be omitted for `/eli5` Quick answers).
- **Update `## Behavioral Rules`** to add an explicit rule: "Lead with the TL;DR. No throat-clearing, no restating the question."
- Preserve all existing content that is not directly replaced (Expertise Domains, Reference Sources, Citation Rules — those stay; Citation Rules get hardened in FEAT-FP-WEX-003).

### Spec-Specific Overrides

No overrides — see memory.

### Out of Scope

- Anti-hallucination rules and citation-format hardening — that is FEAT-FP-WEX-003.
- Wiring `/eli5` and `/deep` commands in `COMMAND_FOCUS` — that is FEAT-FP-WEX-004. This feature only defines the *prompt-side* behavior these commands will eventually trigger.
- Any TypeScript code changes. This is a prompt-content-only feature.
- Wave-Client-specific persona content (deferred — separate agent owns that).

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

N/A — no data model changes.

## 4. Files

- `packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts` — edit the inlined prompt constant only.
- `packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts` — add tests asserting new prompt sections and rules are present.

## 5. Dependencies

- **FEAT-FP-WEX-001** (Inline System Prompt) — must ship first so prompt edits actually reach the LLM.

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Add `## Audience & Depth Tiers` section to the prompt

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Insert a new `## Audience & Depth Tiers` section into the inlined prompt in [packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts](packages/arena/src/agents/webExpertAgent.ts), immediately after `## Identity` and before `## Expertise Domains`.
  - Section content must specify:
    - **Default audience**: software professional with general web literacy.
    - **Three depth tiers**:
      - **Quick** (~3–6 sentences): plain English, minimal jargon, one analogy if helpful, no RFC citations unless trivially relevant. Triggered by `/eli5`, "explain simply", "in plain English", PM/QA framing ("what does X mean", "why would I care").
      - **Default** (the standard 5-section layout): used when no explicit depth signal is present.
      - **Deep** (full spec walkthrough): RFC sections, edge cases, multiple examples, comparison tables. Triggered by `/deep`, "how does X actually work", "walk me through", "what does the spec say".
    - **Switching rules**: explicitly state the model MUST shift tier based on these cues even mid-conversation, and MUST acknowledge the shift briefly ("Going deeper:" / "Quick version:") only on the first message after a switch.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-004.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none (the prompt is the documentation).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena build` succeeds and the new section appears verbatim in the bundled output.

### TASK-002: Replace `## Response Structure` with the standard 5-section layout

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Replace the existing `## Response Structure` section in the inlined prompt with a new specification:
    - **Section order (mandatory unless noted)**:
      1. **TL;DR** — 1 sentence, bold-prefixed `**TL;DR:**`. Always present.
      2. **Answer** — body of the response, organized with `##` subheaders if it covers more than one concept. Always present.
      3. **Example** — fenced code block with language tag (`http`, `bash`, `javascript`, etc.). Omit if the question is purely conceptual.
      4. **Spec reference** — blockquote citing RFC §, W3C spec section, or MDN page. Omit only for `/eli5` Quick tier or for non-protocol questions.
      5. **Key Takeaway** — `---` rule followed by `> **Key Takeaway:** <one sentence>`. Always present.
    - **Tier-specific overrides**:
      - **Quick tier**: TL;DR (the whole response can be just this) + optional Key Takeaway. No Example, no Spec ref required.
      - **Default tier**: all 5 sections, omitting Example only for purely conceptual questions.
      - **Deep tier**: all 5 sections plus may include additional `## ` subsections (Edge Cases, History, Comparison) inside Answer.
    - Preserve and re-cite the existing GFM rules (no raw HTML, fenced code blocks with language tags, GFM tables for comparisons, bold for protocol names/RFC numbers on first use).
  - Move the existing `### Protocol Examples`, `### Comparison Tables`, and `### RFC References` subsections under the new Response Structure section as concrete examples of the Example and Spec-reference parts.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-004.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; the bundled prompt contains the literal strings `**TL;DR:**`, `**Key Takeaway:**`, and the new section ordering.

### TASK-003: Add a "lead with TL;DR" Behavioral Rule

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In the existing `## Behavioral Rules` section of the inlined prompt, add a new numbered rule (insert as rule 1, renumber the rest): "**Lead with the TL;DR.** Open every response with the bolded TL;DR sentence. No throat-clearing ('Great question…'), no restating the user's question, no preamble."
  - Add a companion rule: "**Match depth to the cue.** Before answering, decide which depth tier (Quick / Default / Deep) applies per `## Audience & Depth Tiers`, and structure accordingly."
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: see TASK-004.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: build succeeds; the bundled prompt contains both rule strings.

### TASK-004: Unit tests asserting prompt sections, depth tiers, and rules are present

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In [packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts](packages/arena/src/test/agents/webExpertAgent.test.ts), add a `describe('persona & response-structure prompt content', ...)` block.
  - **Test 1 — Audience tiers section present**: invoke `chat()` with a trivial message, capture the `SystemMessage` passed to the mock LLM, assert `.content` includes `'## Audience & Depth Tiers'`, `'/eli5'`, `'/deep'`, `'Quick'`, `'Default'`, and `'Deep'`.
  - **Test 2 — Response structure template present**: assert the captured SystemMessage `.content` includes `'**TL;DR:**'`, `'**Key Takeaway:**'`, and the section names `'TL;DR'`, `'Answer'`, `'Example'`, `'Spec reference'` in that order (use `indexOf` to assert ordering).
  - **Test 3 — Lead-with-TL;DR rule present**: assert `.content` includes the literal strings `'Lead with the TL;DR'` and `'Match depth to the cue'`.
  - Reuse existing `makeMockLLM()` and `fetchSpy` infrastructure — no new mocking.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: this task *is* the test task.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: none.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/arena test webExpertAgent` passes; all three new tests appear green.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003.

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A.
- **Error Handling**: N/A — prompt-content-only changes; no new fallible operations.
- **Logging**: N/A.
- **Security**: Prompt is static text shipped in the bundle; no secrets, no new attack surface.
- **Performance**: Adds ~80–120 lines to the prompt, increasing per-call input token cost by a small fraction. Acceptable for the audience-coverage gain. No runtime hot-path impact.
- **Backwards compatibility**: Per memory General §3, not required. Existing `systemPrompt` config override remains the escape hatch for any consumer who wants to fully replace the prompt.
